plainEnglish: At Level AA, tap and click targets must be at least 24×24 CSS pixels, or have enough spacing that a 24px circle centered on each doesn't overlap a neighbor. Tiny, crowded controls are hard to hit accurately, especially on touchscreens.
whyItMatters: Touchscreen users and people with motor disabilities or tremors mis-tap controls that are smaller than 24×24px or packed too closely together.
quickCheck: Measure interactive targets in DevTools and confirm each is at least 24×24 CSS pixels or has adequate spacing; axe's target-size rule flags small ones.
commonMistakes: Buttons, links, or icons smaller than 24×24px (axe target-size).
Adjacent small targets with no spacing between them.
Tiny close (×) buttons or icon-only toolbar buttons.
Small inline checkboxes or radios with cramped clickable areas.
